title: crataegus × macrocarpa subsp. calciphila
intro: Crataegus × macrocarpa subsp. calciphila is a hawthorn plant species, a hybrid of Crataegus monogyna and Crataegus laevigata, known for its unique characteristics and medicinal properties. The plant's leaves, fruits, and flowers have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat cardiovascular issues, such as high blood pressure and heart failure, due to their high levels of flavonoids and procyanidins. The health benefits of Crataegus × macrocarpa subsp. calciphila include reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and lowering cholesterol levels, making it a popular herbal remedy for cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and capsules, allowing individuals to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
